[2025-05-14 06:57:16] NEW MESSAGES:

SimpleCoder: 
import os
from PIL import Image

def resize_image(input_file, target_size, output_file):
    # Open the input image
    image = Image.open(input_file)

    # Get the current size of the image
    width, height = image.size

    # Calculate the aspect ratio
    aspect_ratio = width / height

    # Determine the new size based on the target size and aspect ratio
    if target_size[0] is None:
        new_height = target_size[1]
        new_width = int(new_height * aspect_ratio)
    else:
        new_width = target_size[0]
        new_height = int(new_width / aspect_ratio)

    # Resize the image
    resized_image = image.resize((new_width, new_height), Image.ANTIALIAS)

    # Save the resized image to the output file
    resized_image.save(output_file)
    print(f"Image resized and saved to {output_file}")

# Example usage
input_file = "input_image.jpg"
target_size = (800, None)  # specify the desired width or height, or both
output_file = "resized_image.jpg"

resize_image(input_file, target_size, output_file)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SimpleTester: 
import os
import shutil
import pytest
from PIL import Image
from resize_image import resize_image

def test_resize_image_with_width():
    # Prepare test data
    input_file = "test_image.jpg"
    shutil.copy("input_image.jpg", input_file)  # Copy the original file for testing
    target_size = (800, None)
    output_file = "resized_image.jpg"

    # Call the function
    resize_image(input_file, target_size, output_file)

    # Check the resized image
    resized_image = Image.open(output_file)
    assert resized_image.size[0] == 800

    # Clean up
    os.remove(input_file)
    os.remove(output_file)

def test_resize_image_with_height():
    # Prepare test data
    input_file = "test_image.jpg"
    shutil.copy("input_image.jpg", input_file)  # Copy the original file for testing
    target_size = (None, 600)
    output_file = "resized_image.jpg"

    # Call the function
    resize_image(input_file, target_size, output_file)

    # Check the resized image
    resized_image = Image.open(output_file)
    assert resized_image.size[1] == 600

    # Clean up
    os.remove(input_file)
    os.remove(output_file)

def test_resize_image_aspect_ratio():
    # Prepare test data
    input_file = "test_image.jpg"
    shutil.copy("input_image.jpg", input_file)  # Copy the original file for testing
    target_size = (800, None)
    output_file = "resized_image.jpg"

    # Call the function
    resize_image(input_file, target_size, output_file)

    # Check the aspect ratio
    original_image = Image.open(input_file)
    resized_image = Image.open(output_file)
    original_aspect_ratio = original_image.size[0] / original_image.size[1]
    resized_aspect_ratio = resized_image.size[0] / resized_image.size[1]
    assert pytest.approx(original_aspect_ratio, abs=0.01) == resized_aspect_ratio

    # Clean up
    os.remove(input_file)
    os.remove(output_file)

def test_resize_image_invalid_input_file():
    # Prepare test data
    input_file = "invalid_file.jpg"
    target_size = (800, None)
    output_file = "resized_image.jpg"

    # Call the function and check for exception
    with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
        resize_image(input_file, target_size, output_file)

def test_resize_image_invalid_output_file():
    # Prepare test data
    input_file = "input_image.jpg"
    target_size = (800, None)
    output_file = "/invalid/path/resized_image.jpg"

    # Call the function and check for exception
    with pytest.raises(OSError):
        resize_image(input_file, target_size, output_file)
